steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content:
Before the blank: ...his use of graffiti-like scribbles.
After the blank: often incorporate words or phrases... — a verb phrase that needs a subject.

The phrase that often incorporate... is a restrictive relative clause modifying scribbles. No punctuation should separate scribbles from that.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Choice C: scribbles that — joins the noun directly to its restrictive clause with no punctuation, producing a single grammatical sentence.

A and D wrongly insert a semicolon or colon. B creates a sentence fragment beginning with That.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
